tool_input.summary: To study the morphology of Mercury’s “dayside ring” (commonly analyzed as a spatial brightness enhancement in Mercury’s dayside exosphere, especially in Na emission), the most relevant PDS holdings are MESSENGER MASCS/UVVS exosphere observations (PDS3 Atmospheres node) plus MESSENGER SPICE kernels for viewing/illumination geometry. No directly corresponding MASCS exosphere archive was found in PDS4 in this catalog snapshot; PDS4 MESSENGER plasma/field bundles can be used for context (magnetospheric conditions) when interpreting ring morphology.

candidates.title: ATM Volume messmas_2001 (MESSENGER Mascs Calibrated and Derived Data Archive)
candidates.reasoning: MASCS UVVS derived data records are the most direct archive products for mapping/characterizing dayside exospheric emission morphology (e.g., producing lat/long brightness maps and identifying ring-like enhancements).

candidates.title: ATM Volume messmas_2001 (MESSENGER Mascs Calibrated and Derived Data Archive)
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated UVVS spectra support custom derivation of Na (and other species) emission brightness and alternative mapping approaches to assess the dayside ring morphology.

candidates.title: ATM Volume messmas_1001 (MESSENGER Mascs Uncalibrated Data Archive)
candidates.reasoning: Uncalibrated UVVS observations (EDR) are useful if you need to redo calibration/processing choices that affect inferred ring morphology (background subtraction, line fitting, stray light handling).

candidates.title: ATM Volume messmas_2101 (MESSENGER Mascs Calibrated and Derived Data Archive)
candidates.reasoning: Combined UVVS/VIRS derived products may help cross-check observation context (simultaneous MASCS channels) when constructing dayside exosphere morphology products.

candidates.title: MESSENGER SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels are needed to compute Mercury-local time, solar zenith angle, and spacecraft viewing geometry for each UVVS line-of-sight—critical for converting observations into a dayside ‘ring’ morphology map.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Use calibrated magnetic-field time series to relate observed dayside-ring morphology to magnetospheric forcing (e.g., cusp precipitation / IMF conditions) during MASCS exosphere observations.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived ion/plasma products can provide upstream/interaction context that may correlate with when/where dayside ring-like exospheric enhancements form.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
